attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

lastPrompt: draw the core architecture of hybrid_sim. explain the core components of both QEMU and verilator.  explain the architecture transportation in between in an inteactive way.  create a html file to compl…

content.content:
1	// V-register transport via shared scratch RAM. The vsim Debug Module
2	// abstract-command space does not allocate slots for v0..v31 (RISC-V Debug
3	// Spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer or implementation-specific
4	// extensions), and the AX45MPV CPU wrapper does not export a DPI probe for
5	// the V regfile. As a stop-gap that keeps Phase 5 (DPI / PB) deferred while
6	// still letting C-level RVV fixtures round-trip:
7	//
8	//   - On resume from QEMU 1, vsim copies state.vreg[] into a fixed scratch
9	//     region of guest RAM (kVScratchGpa). The fixture's vsim-phase prologue
10	//     issues `vle8.v vN, (t0)` against that region to repopulate v0..v31.
11	//   - Before the vsim->QEMU2 drain trigger, the fixture issues `vse8.v vN,
12	//     (t0)` to write the (possibly mutated) regfile back to the same region.
13	//     vsim's StateDrain side reads the scratch bytes into state.vreg[] so
14	//     qemu_handback can ship them to QEMU 2 via the existing gdbstub P
15	//     packets.
16	//
17	// The contract is fixture-aware: any program that wants V state preserved
18	// across the vsim boundary must perform the load/store at the boundaries.
19	// rt_c_v_regs.c demonstrates the pattern. Phase 5 will replace this with a
20	// transparent mechanism (PB or DPI) so benchmark binaries don't need to be
21	// modified.
22	//
23	// Pure logic: no SystemC, no DMI bus. Easy to unit-test.
24	
25	#pragma once
26	
27	#include "hybrid/state_abi.h"
28	
29	#include <cstddef>
30	#include <cstdint>
31	#include <cstring>
32	#include <span>
33	#include <stdexcept>
34	
35	namespace hybrid
36	{
37	
38	// Reserved scratch slot inside the shared mmap. Sits well above the C
39	// runtime's _stack (rt_c.ld places it at RAM_BASE + 0x700000), giving the
40	// stack ~9 MiB of headroom before colliding with this 2 KiB region. On
41	// `-M andes_ae350` RAM is at 0x00000000 and the GPA is 0x00FF0000; on
42	// `-M virt` it would be 0x80FF0000. The chosen address is RAM_BASE +
43	// 0xFF0000, an offset that has to match the `li t0, ...` constant in the
44	// fixture's runtime/v_regfile_xfer.c.
45	inline constexpr uint64_t kVScratchGpa = 0x00FF0000ull;
46	
47	// Total scratch bytes needed: 32 V regs × HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Sized to
48	// the maximum supported VLEN; the actual per-reg byte count is `vlenb`.
49	inline constexpr std::size_t kVScratchBytes =
50	  static_cast<std::size_t>(32) * HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX;
51	
52	namespace detail
53	{
54	
55	// Translate scratch GPA into a byte offset inside a RAM span whose first
56	// byte sits at `ram_base`. Throws if the scratch region falls outside the
57	// supplied span (i.e. the caller passed a too-small RAM mapping).
58	inline auto scratch_offset(std::size_t ram_size, uint64_t ram_base)
59	  -> std::size_t
60	{
61	  if (kVScratchGpa < ram_base)
62	    throw std::runtime_error(
63	      "v_regfile_xfer: scratch GPA below RAM base");
64	  const auto off = static_cast<std::size_t>(kVScratchGpa - ram_base);
65	  if (off + kVScratchBytes > ram_size)
66	    throw std::runtime_error(
67	      "v_regfile_xfer: scratch region exceeds RAM span");
68	  return off;
69	}
70	
71	}  // namespace detail
72	
73	// Copy state.vreg[] into ram[scratch_offset .. scratch_offset+kVScratchBytes].
74	// Called just before vsim resumes the hart so the fixture can vle8.v from
75	// scratch and pick up QEMU 1's V values.
76	inline void stage_v_to_scratch(const hybrid_state_v1& state,
77	                               std::span<uint8_t> ram,
78	                               uint64_t ram_base)
79	{
80	  const auto off = detail::scratch_offset(ram.size(), ram_base);
81	  std::memcpy(ram.data() + off, state.vreg, kVScratchBytes);
82	}
83	
84	// Copy ram[scratch_offset .. +kVScratchBytes] into state.vreg[]. Called
85	// inside the vsim drain path after the fixture's prologue vse8.v has
86	// settled the (possibly mutated) regfile bytes into RAM.
87	inline void read_v_from_scratch(hybrid_state_v1& state,
88	                                std::span<const uint8_t> ram,
89	                                uint64_t ram_base)
90	{
91	  const auto off = detail::scratch_offset(ram.size(), ram_base);
92	  std::memcpy(state.vreg, ram.data() + off, kVScratchBytes);
93	}
94	
95	}  // namespace hybrid
96

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/gdbstub_client.hpp
hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_loader.hpp
hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.hpp
hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/gdbstub_session.hpp

content.text: I have enough context. I'll create a self-contained interactive HTML file in `docs/`.

input.content:
hybrid_sim - Core Architecture

Andes RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary.
QEMU (TCG functional) vsim / Verilator (cycle-accurate) Shared ABI / transport

1. Three-phase round-trip

Execution starts in QEMU for fast forward, hands off to vsim at a trigger for the cycle-accurate region of interest, then hands back to a fresh QEMU to finish. Click each box to see what it contains.

Phase 1 - functional
QEMU 1
  • TCG plugin matches entry trigger
  • Drains GPR/FPR/CSR/V from gdbstub
  • Writes hybrid_state_v1, exits
hybrid_state_v1 state file (atomic write)
Phase 2 - cycle-accurate
vsim (Verilator)
  • HandoffController orchestrates
  • ResumeDriver restores via DM
  • RTL runs; StateDrain captures on exit
hybrid_state_v1 gdbstub P-packets
Phase 3 - functional
QEMU 2
  • Spawned with -gdb -S
  • State restored via P-packets
  • Continues; exit code propagates

Click a phase above to inspect its internals.

Each phase exchanges the same hybrid_state_v1 struct (defined in include/hybrid/state_abi.h), but uses a completely different transport at each boundary: a binary file (QEMU1 to vsim) and the GDB Remote Serial Protocol (vsim to QEMU2). The shared mmap (/dev/shm) is also live in tier-2 fixtures so the C heap/stack survives across all three phases.

2. Core components

Click each card to expand. The QEMU side is one C plugin loaded into upstream QEMU; the vsim side is a set of header-only C++20 classes orchestrated by a simulator main loop.

QEMU qemu_plugin / hybrid_handoff.c - drain on trigger

Trigger detection

Translation-time callback (vcpu_tb_trans) inspects every 4-byte insn in the block.

  • CSR mode (default): matches csrwi 0x7C0, 0 for enter and csrwi 0x7C0, 1 for exit. CSR 0x7C0 is a vendor write that has no architectural effect on Andes ax45mp - hence the "magic" trigger.
  • PC mode: when the plugin is loaded with enter_pc=0x..., the CSR matcher is bypassed and only that exact vaddr fires (works on compressed insns via qemu_plugin_insn_size).

Register harvest

On trigger, resolve_handles() looks up every gdbstub register by name. The lookup is lazy because QEMU only registers the dynamic coprocessor features (FPU, V, CSR) after vcpu_init.

  • 32 GPR + 32 FPR + PC
  • 13 M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ..., satp)
  • 16 pmpcfg + 64 pmpaddr
  • V CSRs (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) + v0..v31 (bytes)

PC sourcing quirk

The PC value comes from compute_handoff_pc(site) using the translation-time vaddr + insn size, not from the gdbstub PC register. Reason: TCG defers PC writes - after a V-extension instruction retires the live gdbstub PC can briefly lag the actual current insn.

Atomic state write

write_state_atomic() writes to $outfile.tmp.$pid, fsyncs, then rename()s. The vsim side never sees a torn struct. After write, QEMU calls exit(0).

qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c, include/hybrid/insn_match.h, include/hybrid/state_abi.h
vsim HandoffController - phase dispatcher

Role

Glues CsrTrigger (decoder of magic CSRs) and StateDrain. The Verilator run-loop calls on_retire(pc, gpr_probe) once per committed PC.

Decisions

  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER - QEMU's job, vsim ignores
  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT - fire drain
  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME - read rs1 GPR, fire only if value==1

Passthrough merge

The vsim DM cannot read every CSR/V byte cheaply. set_passthrough_input(qemu1_state) caches the QEMU1 snapshot; on drain, drain_with_passthrough overwrites only the fresh fields (GPRs, PC, six V CSRs) and keeps the rest as-is.

Exit code

When drain fires, the simulator writes the state file and exits with EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU. The main() wrapper interprets that and invokes run_qemu_handback.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp
vsim ResumeDriver - restore state via Debug Module

Transport: RISC-V Debug Module

The vsim CPU model exposes a Debug Module over a DMI bus. ResumeDriver only knows about a 32-bit register interface (DmiBus::write/read) - SystemC details are not in this header. This makes the class trivially unit-testable with a fake bus.

Two-tier CSR restore

  • Tier A - Abstract Command writes for GPRs, DPC, mstatus.VS, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr.
  • Tier B - Program Buffer for V config: stage vl in x5, vtype in x6, then run vsetvl x0, x5, x6. Direct csrrw to vtype would set vill; vl is read-only.

Strict ordering

Order matters because of side effects:

  1. dmactive 0 to 1, halt, wait allhalted
  2. mstatus.VS (enables V in hart)
  3. vsetvl via PB (resets vstart to 0)
  4. vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr (AFTER vsetvl)
  5. GPR loop x1..x31, DPC (overwrites x5/x6 scratch - safe now)
  6. mtvec tolerant, clear cmderr, resumereq

cmderr tolerance

The Debug Spec makes abstractcs.cmderr sticky. A failed write of an unimplemented CSR would block every subsequent command. The driver checks cmderr after each write, W1C-clears it, and either retries via Program Buffer (write_csr_abstract_or_pb) or treats the slot as a no-op (write_csr_tolerant).

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp, abstract_command.hpp, program_buffer.hpp, dmi_poll.hpp
vsim StateDrain - capture on exit trigger

What it reads

Reuses ResumeDriver's DM constants. Halts the hart, reads x1..x31 + DPC via Abstract Commands. FPR/CSR/PMP/V byte arrays are not re-read from the DM - they come from the cached QEMU1 snapshot.

drain_with_passthrough

When HYBRID_FLAG_V is set, also freshly reads the six scalar V CSRs (vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vtype, vl). The 32 V data registers come back via the shared scratch RAM, not via DM.

Error model

If the DM raises cmderr on a register read, that field is reported as 0 after a W1C clear, and the next read proceeds. A missing CSR mirrors as zero rather than corrupting the adjacent slot.

Pure-logic split

The class only touches a DmiBus&. Tests use recording_bus.hpp doctest fixtures to verify the exact DMI write/read sequence without needing SystemC or Verilator.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp
vsim qemu_handback - spawn QEMU 2 and replay via gdbstub

Spawn

spawn_qemu() forks, redirects stdin to /dev/null (so QEMU's tcsetattr can't SIGTTOU the parent), and execs:

qemu-system-riscv64 \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -gdb tcp::PORT -S \
  [tier-1] -kernel ELF \
  [tier-2] -object memory-backend-file,share=on,... -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem

Register-map discovery

discover_reg_map() walks target.xml and every xi:include annex via qXfer:features:read (chunked because the CSR XML is ~6 KB and PacketSize is ~1000). The result is a register-name to packet-number map - the same names the QEMU plugin used on phase 1.

State application

apply_state() sends P-packets for every field. Each call is silently skipped if the register name is not advertised (some QEMU builds omit PMP slots or extension CSRs).

  • x1..x31 + PC (hard-coded numbers 1..32)
  • FPR f0..f31 (if FP flag)
  • 13 M-mode CSRs + 16 pmpcfg + 64 pmpaddr
  • v0..v31 as vlenb-byte payloads + 6 V CSRs (if V flag)

Continue and propagate

After sess.cont(), the parent waitpid()s and returns QEMU2's exit code. If QEMU2 dies before the gdbstub port becomes reachable (bad --qemu-binary path), ChildExitedDuringConnect surfaces that status instead of hanging.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp, gdbstub_session.hpp, reg_names.h
ABI hybrid_state_v1 - the wire contract

Single source of truth

include/hybrid/state_abi.h defines the binary layout. Both the QEMU plugin (C) and vsim (C++) include this header. Layout is locked by state_abi_check.h static_asserts.

Versioning

  • Magic: 0x42594841 ('AHYB')
  • Current version: 1
  • Forward-compat: never repurpose offsets in v1 - grow via reserved[232] or introduce v2 alongside.

Flags

  • HYBRID_FLAG_RV64 - always set
  • HYBRID_FLAG_FP - set only if QEMU advertised D-extension and FPR handles bound
  • HYBRID_FLAG_V - set only if QEMU exposed vlenb > 0

Receivers MUST check flags before consuming the matching state blocks.

Sizing

HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX = 64 (VLEN=512). Driven by NDS_VLEN in data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg - changing the configured VLEN means updating this constant in lock-step.

include/hybrid/state_abi.h, include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h
ABI V-state transport - the trickiest piece

Why scalar V CSRs are split

Tier A (Abstract): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS - plain CSR writes work because they have no architectural side effect on vtype/vl.

Tier B (Program Buffer): vtype and vl must come from vsetvl x0, x5, x6. A direct csrrw to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only outside of vsetvl. Only vsetvl restores both atomically.

Why V data regs need a scratch region

The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate Abstract Command slots for v0..v31, and the AX45MPV CPU wrapper exports no DPI probe for the V regfile.

Workaround: a reserved 2 KiB region at kVScratchGpa inside the shared mmap. The fixture's prologue executes vle8.v vN, (t0) from scratch on entry; epilogue vse8.v vN, (t0) on exit. vsim reads the bytes back from the host-mapped pointer (read_v_from_scratch).

kVScratchGpa selection

0x00FF0000 on -M andes_ae350, 0x80FF0000 on -M virt. Chosen as RAM_BASE + 0xFF0000 so the C stack (rt_c.ld places _stack at RAM_BASE + 0x700000) has ~9 MiB of headroom before colliding.

Restore ordering recap

The strict ResumeDriver order exists because every step depends on the previous one.

mstatus.VS = state.mstatus & 0x600
write x5 = state.vl     (Abstract)
write x6 = state.vtype  (Abstract)
PB: vsetvl x0, x5, x6   (atomic vtype+vl; resets vstart)
vstart = state.vstart
vxsat / vxrm / vcsr
GPRs x1..x31 (overwrites x5/x6)
DPC = state.pc
mtvec, resume
hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp, resume_driver.hpp; fixtures: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_*.c

3. Interactive state transport

Step through the round-trip. Each step lights up the actors involved and shows what data moves on what wire.

QEMU 1 process
qemu-system-riscv64 + libhybrid_handoff.so
|
state file (hybrid_state_v1)
written atomically, ~12 KB binary
|
vsim process (Verilator)
HandoffController + DMI + RTL hart
|
/dev/shm shared mmap (tier-2)
.text + .data + .bss + V scratch
|
QEMU 2 process
gdbstub on tcp::PORT, halted at -S

4. The hybrid_state_v1 ABI

Same struct, three traversals. Total size is fixed across versions; reserved bytes absorb additions.

Header 24 B

  • magic - 'AHYB' (0x42594841)
  • version - 1
  • flags - RV64 | FP | V
  • hart_id, pc, priv
Receiver validates magic + version first; otherwise refuses.

GPR + FPR 512 B

  • gpr[32] - x0..x31 (x0 always 0)
  • fpr[32] - f0..f31, valid only if HYBRID_FLAG_FP
Restored via DM Abstract Commands (vsim) and gdbstub P-packets (QEMU2).

M-mode CSRs 104 B

  • mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch
  • medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp
vsim restores only what the DM accepts; cmderr-tolerant.

PMP 640 B

  • pmpcfg[16] - 16 8-byte slots (works for RV64 8/CSR or future RV32 4/CSR)
  • pmpaddr[64] - 64 region bases

V scalar CSRs 32 B

  • vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb
  • vxsat, vxrm, vcsr
vtype + vl restored together via vsetvl in Program Buffer; others via Abstract.

V data regs 2048 B

  • vreg[32 * 64] - up to VLEN=512 bits each
DM has no slot for V regs - transported via shared scratch RAM and fixture-side vle8.v/vse8.v. QEMU2 gets them as P-packet byte payloads.

reserved 232 B

  • Zero-initialised on write
  • Sized to keep total layout stable
v2-style additions: consume bytes here without renumbering existing offsets.

Triggers (not in struct)

  • CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 enter / csrwi 0x7C0, 1 exit
  • PC mode: ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc
CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp - architecturally inert, so dropping it from real workloads is free.

5. Source map

include/hybrid/state_abi.h
Shared wire-format struct (C and C++)
qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c
QEMU TCG plugin: detect trigger, drain state
.../src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp
vsim phase dispatcher
.../src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp
DM restore (Tier A + Tier B)
.../src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp
DM capture on exit trigger
.../src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp
Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets
.../src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp
V regfile transport via shared mmap scratch
.../src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp
Trigger insn pattern map
.../src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp
DM Abstract Command encoder
.../src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp
DM Program Buffer helpers
.../cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
CTest registration for e2e fixtures
run_e2e.sh
Parallel e2e runner
hybrid_sim - generated locally for navigation; not auto-updated. Open this file in any browser.

toolUseResult.content:
hybrid_sim - Core Architecture

Andes RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary.
QEMU (TCG functional) vsim / Verilator (cycle-accurate) Shared ABI / transport

1. Three-phase round-trip

Execution starts in QEMU for fast forward, hands off to vsim at a trigger for the cycle-accurate region of interest, then hands back to a fresh QEMU to finish. Click each box to see what it contains.

Phase 1 - functional
QEMU 1
  • TCG plugin matches entry trigger
  • Drains GPR/FPR/CSR/V from gdbstub
  • Writes hybrid_state_v1, exits
hybrid_state_v1 state file (atomic write)
Phase 2 - cycle-accurate
vsim (Verilator)
  • HandoffController orchestrates
  • ResumeDriver restores via DM
  • RTL runs; StateDrain captures on exit
hybrid_state_v1 gdbstub P-packets
Phase 3 - functional
QEMU 2
  • Spawned with -gdb -S
  • State restored via P-packets
  • Continues; exit code propagates

Click a phase above to inspect its internals.

Each phase exchanges the same hybrid_state_v1 struct (defined in include/hybrid/state_abi.h), but uses a completely different transport at each boundary: a binary file (QEMU1 to vsim) and the GDB Remote Serial Protocol (vsim to QEMU2). The shared mmap (/dev/shm) is also live in tier-2 fixtures so the C heap/stack survives across all three phases.

2. Core components

Click each card to expand. The QEMU side is one C plugin loaded into upstream QEMU; the vsim side is a set of header-only C++20 classes orchestrated by a simulator main loop.

QEMU qemu_plugin / hybrid_handoff.c - drain on trigger

Trigger detection

Translation-time callback (vcpu_tb_trans) inspects every 4-byte insn in the block.

  • CSR mode (default): matches csrwi 0x7C0, 0 for enter and csrwi 0x7C0, 1 for exit. CSR 0x7C0 is a vendor write that has no architectural effect on Andes ax45mp - hence the "magic" trigger.
  • PC mode: when the plugin is loaded with enter_pc=0x..., the CSR matcher is bypassed and only that exact vaddr fires (works on compressed insns via qemu_plugin_insn_size).

Register harvest

On trigger, resolve_handles() looks up every gdbstub register by name. The lookup is lazy because QEMU only registers the dynamic coprocessor features (FPU, V, CSR) after vcpu_init.

  • 32 GPR + 32 FPR + PC
  • 13 M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ..., satp)
  • 16 pmpcfg + 64 pmpaddr
  • V CSRs (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) + v0..v31 (bytes)

PC sourcing quirk

The PC value comes from compute_handoff_pc(site) using the translation-time vaddr + insn size, not from the gdbstub PC register. Reason: TCG defers PC writes - after a V-extension instruction retires the live gdbstub PC can briefly lag the actual current insn.

Atomic state write

write_state_atomic() writes to $outfile.tmp.$pid, fsyncs, then rename()s. The vsim side never sees a torn struct. After write, QEMU calls exit(0).

qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c, include/hybrid/insn_match.h, include/hybrid/state_abi.h
vsim HandoffController - phase dispatcher

Role

Glues CsrTrigger (decoder of magic CSRs) and StateDrain. The Verilator run-loop calls on_retire(pc, gpr_probe) once per committed PC.

Decisions

  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER - QEMU's job, vsim ignores
  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT - fire drain
  • HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME - read rs1 GPR, fire only if value==1

Passthrough merge

The vsim DM cannot read every CSR/V byte cheaply. set_passthrough_input(qemu1_state) caches the QEMU1 snapshot; on drain, drain_with_passthrough overwrites only the fresh fields (GPRs, PC, six V CSRs) and keeps the rest as-is.

Exit code

When drain fires, the simulator writes the state file and exits with EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU. The main() wrapper interprets that and invokes run_qemu_handback.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp
vsim ResumeDriver - restore state via Debug Module

Transport: RISC-V Debug Module

The vsim CPU model exposes a Debug Module over a DMI bus. ResumeDriver only knows about a 32-bit register interface (DmiBus::write/read) - SystemC details are not in this header. This makes the class trivially unit-testable with a fake bus.

Two-tier CSR restore

  • Tier A - Abstract Command writes for GPRs, DPC, mstatus.VS, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr.
  • Tier B - Program Buffer for V config: stage vl in x5, vtype in x6, then run vsetvl x0, x5, x6. Direct csrrw to vtype would set vill; vl is read-only.

Strict ordering

Order matters because of side effects:

  1. dmactive 0 to 1, halt, wait allhalted
  2. mstatus.VS (enables V in hart)
  3. vsetvl via PB (resets vstart to 0)
  4. vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr (AFTER vsetvl)
  5. GPR loop x1..x31, DPC (overwrites x5/x6 scratch - safe now)
  6. mtvec tolerant, clear cmderr, resumereq

cmderr tolerance

The Debug Spec makes abstractcs.cmderr sticky. A failed write of an unimplemented CSR would block every subsequent command. The driver checks cmderr after each write, W1C-clears it, and either retries via Program Buffer (write_csr_abstract_or_pb) or treats the slot as a no-op (write_csr_tolerant).

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp, abstract_command.hpp, program_buffer.hpp, dmi_poll.hpp
vsim StateDrain - capture on exit trigger

What it reads

Reuses ResumeDriver's DM constants. Halts the hart, reads x1..x31 + DPC via Abstract Commands. FPR/CSR/PMP/V byte arrays are not re-read from the DM - they come from the cached QEMU1 snapshot.

drain_with_passthrough

When HYBRID_FLAG_V is set, also freshly reads the six scalar V CSRs (vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vtype, vl). The 32 V data registers come back via the shared scratch RAM, not via DM.

Error model

If the DM raises cmderr on a register read, that field is reported as 0 after a W1C clear, and the next read proceeds. A missing CSR mirrors as zero rather than corrupting the adjacent slot.

Pure-logic split

The class only touches a DmiBus&. Tests use recording_bus.hpp doctest fixtures to verify the exact DMI write/read sequence without needing SystemC or Verilator.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp
vsim qemu_handback - spawn QEMU 2 and replay via gdbstub

Spawn

spawn_qemu() forks, redirects stdin to /dev/null (so QEMU's tcsetattr can't SIGTTOU the parent), and execs:

qemu-system-riscv64 \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -gdb tcp::PORT -S \
  [tier-1] -kernel ELF \
  [tier-2] -object memory-backend-file,share=on,... -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem

Register-map discovery

discover_reg_map() walks target.xml and every xi:include annex via qXfer:features:read (chunked because the CSR XML is ~6 KB and PacketSize is ~1000). The result is a register-name to packet-number map - the same names the QEMU plugin used on phase 1.

State application

apply_state() sends P-packets for every field. Each call is silently skipped if the register name is not advertised (some QEMU builds omit PMP slots or extension CSRs).

  • x1..x31 + PC (hard-coded numbers 1..32)
  • FPR f0..f31 (if FP flag)
  • 13 M-mode CSRs + 16 pmpcfg + 64 pmpaddr
  • v0..v31 as vlenb-byte payloads + 6 V CSRs (if V flag)

Continue and propagate

After sess.cont(), the parent waitpid()s and returns QEMU2's exit code. If QEMU2 dies before the gdbstub port becomes reachable (bad --qemu-binary path), ChildExitedDuringConnect surfaces that status instead of hanging.

hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp, gdbstub_session.hpp, reg_names.h
ABI hybrid_state_v1 - the wire contract

Single source of truth

include/hybrid/state_abi.h defines the binary layout. Both the QEMU plugin (C) and vsim (C++) include this header. Layout is locked by state_abi_check.h static_asserts.

Versioning

  • Magic: 0x42594841 ('AHYB')
  • Current version: 1
  • Forward-compat: never repurpose offsets in v1 - grow via reserved[232] or introduce v2 alongside.

Flags

  • HYBRID_FLAG_RV64 - always set
  • HYBRID_FLAG_FP - set only if QEMU advertised D-extension and FPR handles bound
  • HYBRID_FLAG_V - set only if QEMU exposed vlenb > 0

Receivers MUST check flags before consuming the matching state blocks.

Sizing

HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX = 64 (VLEN=512). Driven by NDS_VLEN in data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg - changing the configured VLEN means updating this constant in lock-step.

include/hybrid/state_abi.h, include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h
ABI V-state transport - the trickiest piece

Why scalar V CSRs are split

Tier A (Abstract): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS - plain CSR writes work because they have no architectural side effect on vtype/vl.

Tier B (Program Buffer): vtype and vl must come from vsetvl x0, x5, x6. A direct csrrw to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only outside of vsetvl. Only vsetvl restores both atomically.

Why V data regs need a scratch region

The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate Abstract Command slots for v0..v31, and the AX45MPV CPU wrapper exports no DPI probe for the V regfile.

Workaround: a reserved 2 KiB region at kVScratchGpa inside the shared mmap. The fixture's prologue executes vle8.v vN, (t0) from scratch on entry; epilogue vse8.v vN, (t0) on exit. vsim reads the bytes back from the host-mapped pointer (read_v_from_scratch).

kVScratchGpa selection

0x00FF0000 on -M andes_ae350, 0x80FF0000 on -M virt. Chosen as RAM_BASE + 0xFF0000 so the C stack (rt_c.ld places _stack at RAM_BASE + 0x700000) has ~9 MiB of headroom before colliding.

Restore ordering recap

The strict ResumeDriver order exists because every step depends on the previous one.

mstatus.VS = state.mstatus & 0x600
write x5 = state.vl     (Abstract)
write x6 = state.vtype  (Abstract)
PB: vsetvl x0, x5, x6   (atomic vtype+vl; resets vstart)
vstart = state.vstart
vxsat / vxrm / vcsr
GPRs x1..x31 (overwrites x5/x6)
DPC = state.pc
mtvec, resume
hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp, resume_driver.hpp; fixtures: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_*.c

3. Interactive state transport

Step through the round-trip. Each step lights up the actors involved and shows what data moves on what wire.

QEMU 1 process
qemu-system-riscv64 + libhybrid_handoff.so
|
state file (hybrid_state_v1)
written atomically, ~12 KB binary
|
vsim process (Verilator)
HandoffController + DMI + RTL hart
|
/dev/shm shared mmap (tier-2)
.text + .data + .bss + V scratch
|
QEMU 2 process
gdbstub on tcp::PORT, halted at -S

4. The hybrid_state_v1 ABI

Same struct, three traversals. Total size is fixed across versions; reserved bytes absorb additions.

Header 24 B

  • magic - 'AHYB' (0x42594841)
  • version - 1
  • flags - RV64 | FP | V
  • hart_id, pc, priv
Receiver validates magic + version first; otherwise refuses.

GPR + FPR 512 B

  • gpr[32] - x0..x31 (x0 always 0)
  • fpr[32] - f0..f31, valid only if HYBRID_FLAG_FP
Restored via DM Abstract Commands (vsim) and gdbstub P-packets (QEMU2).

M-mode CSRs 104 B

  • mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch
  • medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp
vsim restores only what the DM accepts; cmderr-tolerant.

PMP 640 B

  • pmpcfg[16] - 16 8-byte slots (works for RV64 8/CSR or future RV32 4/CSR)
  • pmpaddr[64] - 64 region bases

V scalar CSRs 32 B

  • vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb
  • vxsat, vxrm, vcsr
vtype + vl restored together via vsetvl in Program Buffer; others via Abstract.

V data regs 2048 B

  • vreg[32 * 64] - up to VLEN=512 bits each
DM has no slot for V regs - transported via shared scratch RAM and fixture-side vle8.v/vse8.v. QEMU2 gets them as P-packet byte payloads.

reserved 232 B

  • Zero-initialised on write
  • Sized to keep total layout stable
v2-style additions: consume bytes here without renumbering existing offsets.

Triggers (not in struct)

  • CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 enter / csrwi 0x7C0, 1 exit
  • PC mode: ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc
CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp - architecturally inert, so dropping it from real workloads is free.

5. Source map

include/hybrid/state_abi.h
Shared wire-format struct (C and C++)
qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c
QEMU TCG plugin: detect trigger, drain state
.../src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp
vsim phase dispatcher
.../src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp
DM restore (Tier A + Tier B)
.../src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp
DM capture on exit trigger
.../src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp
Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets
.../src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp
V regfile transport via shared mmap scratch
.../src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp
Trigger insn pattern map
.../src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp
DM Abstract Command encoder
.../src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp
DM Program Buffer helpers
.../cmake/HybridConfig.cmake
CTest registration for e2e fixtures
run_e2e.sh
Parallel e2e runner

lastPrompt: draw the core architecture of hybrid_sim. explain the core components of both QEMU and verilator.  explain the architecture transportation in between in an inteactive way.  create a html file to compl…

content.text:
Created `docs/architecture.html` (single self-contained 48 KB file, no external assets).

**Contents:**
1. **Three-phase diagram** - click QEMU1 / vsim / QEMU2 to see per-phase internals
2. **Component cards** (accordion) - QEMU plugin, HandoffController, ResumeDriver, StateDrain, qemu_handback, hybrid_state_v1 ABI, V-state transport
3. **Interactive walkthrough** - 11 steps with Prev/Next, lighting up the actors involved on a left-side stage (QEMU1 -> state file -> vsim -> /dev/shm mmap -> QEMU2), each step shows "What happens" + "Why this way" + code snippet + file paths
4. **ABI viewer** - struct broken into 8 blocks with field-level breakdown
5. **Source map**

Open with `xdg-open docs/architecture.html` or any browser. Dark theme, color-coded (blue=QEMU, green=vsim, orange=shared ABI).
